column2: The night is completely silent. A wide dark river at night, no streetlights, no people, faint ripples of moving water suggested with a few flowing lines, distant bare hills as a thin horizon, total emptiness and isolation. A single small pale-blue accent shimmer on the water.

A lone 13-year-old girl Yeonmi seen from behind, standing small at the edge of a dark riverbank, facing into vast empty darkness, slight tension in her posture. Huge negative space around her to emphasize how alone and exposed she is. A single thin teal accent line for the water at her feet.

Close three-quarter view of Yeonmi's determined frightened face, jaw set, eyes wide and resolute. A single thin red line like a tightrope path stretches across the lower frame with a tiny figure balancing on it, symbolising one mistake means losing everything. The hand-lettered word YEONMI sketched neatly in a corner.

A small bird inside a cage, wings slightly open but trapped, symbolising no freedom. Behind it a faint outline map shape of the Korean peninsula with the northern half lightly hatched. One red accent on the cage bars.

Yeonmi with one hand pressed over her own mouth silencing herself, and beside her a small old TV showing a crossed-out screen, symbolising you cannot say what you think or watch what you want. A single red accent on the X.

A single empty rice bowl with chopsticks resting on a bare wooden table, nothing inside it, symbolising scarcity and not having enough food. A small pair of empty hands reaches toward it from the side. A single small red accent on the bowl rim. Lots of empty white space.

A long line of identical people standing obediently in a queue, heads bowed, uniform posture, faces blank, symbolising that hunger, fear and obedience have become normal. One red accent on a single figure who is faintly lifting their head.
Yeonmi's father being arrested, taken away by two stern guards gripping his arms, his head turned back. Young Yeonmi watches helplessly in the foreground. A single red accent on a guard's armband.
A man secretly trading goods and cash in the shadows of a small black-market stall at night, furtive and hunched, glancing over his shoulder. A single red accent on the folded money in his hand.

An empty wooden chair where the father used to sit, and a faint fading dotted outline of a man dissolving into nothing beside it, symbolising he is suddenly gone with no explanation. A single red accent on the empty chair.
Yeonmi and her mother sitting silently at a bare table with empty plates between them, heads low, heavy silence. Generous empty white space. A single muted red accent on one empty plate.
A cold empty winter street, a few hunched distant figures, one of them faint and fading away, snow suggested only by the white space, no one acknowledging it. A single teal accent for the cold.

Yeonmi's profile lost in thought, and floating near her head a small sketched open door with light spilling through, representing the dangerous idea of escape. The faint hand-lettered word ESCAPE near the door. A single red accent on the doorway.
Yeonmi and her mother facing each other in a quiet resolute moment of decision, a firm nod between them. The small hand-lettered year 2007 in a corner. A single red accent.

A simple hand-drawn map diagram: the Yalu River separating two banks labelled North Korea and China, with a bold curved arrow crossing from one side to the other. The crossing arrow is the single red accent.

An ominous prison-camp watchtower and barbed-wire fence silhouette looming in cold winter emptiness, representing the threat of labor camps if caught. A single red accent on the watchtower searchlight.
A wide view of a partially frozen river, large cracked plates of ice with dark water showing between them, bleak and dangerous, their only chance. A teal accent on the ice.
Foreground of cracked thin ice, and in the far background a small border-guard silhouette sweeping a flashlight. Tension. A single red accent on the flashlight beam.
Yeonmi frozen mid-step on the ice, exaggerated concentric sound ripples spreading from her foot to show how every step felt too loud in the overwhelming silence. A single red accent on the ripples.
Yeonmi and her mother stepping carefully onto the frozen river, holding onto each other, small shiver lines around them showing the biting cold cutting through their bodies. A teal cold accent.
A sharp jagged crack shooting across the ice directly beneath Yeonmi's feet as she freezes wide-eyed in terror, hands out for balance. The crack line is the single red accent.
Yeonmi exhaling in relief as the ice holds beneath her, a small steadying breath, a tiny calm beat of survival. A single soft teal accent.
Yeonmi and her mother reaching the far riverbank into China, but a faint question mark hovers above to hint that this is not real freedom. A single red accent on the question mark.
Yeonmi crouching and hiding in shadow while a searching figure with a sweeping spotlight hunts for her, predator and prey, because refugees are hunted in China. A single red accent on the spotlight.
A heavy ominous arrow pointing backward across the border, dark and dreadful, representing being sent back to the worst fate imaginable. A single red accent on the arrow.
Shadowy menacing smuggler figures looming over a small Yeonmi and her mother, a different kind of nightmare beginning. A single red accent.
Yeonmi and her mother standing with price tags hanging on them as if they were goods for sale, no longer people but commodities. A single red accent on the price tags.
Yeonmi and her mother being pulled apart in opposite directions by grasping hands, a desperate reach between them as they are separated. A single red accent on the reaching hands.
Money changing hands above a small huddled figure of 13-year-old Yeonmi, symbolising she is sold like property to a man. A single red accent on the money.
Yeonmi curled up small in a dark corner, with faint torn calendar pages drifting around her to show years passing while hidden, silent and always afraid. A single muted red accent.
Yeonmi looking up shocked and hopeful as her gaunt, thin father appears unexpectedly in a doorway, having survived the camps and reached China. A single red accent in the doorway.
A small family of three briefly together again, but the father is hunched and frail, coughing, clearly very sick, fragile hope. A single red accent.
The father is gone, and Yeonmi stands alone with a large hand-lettered question mark above her head, facing the question of what now. A single red accent on the question mark.
Yeonmi and her mother shouldering small bundles and walking onward with grim determination, they have to keep moving farther from danger. A single red accent.
A hand-drawn map diagram showing a long route arrow travelling through Mongolia down to South Korea, one of the most dangerous routes. The route arrow is the single red accent.
Two tiny figures crossing a vast empty freezing desert and barren mountains, dwarfed by the immense emptiness, their only chance. A teal accent for the cold.
Yeonmi and her mother travelling at night under a sky of stars and a thin moon, two small figures moving through darkness, hiding by day. A single pale accent on the moon.
Two figures trudging forward with exhausted, shivering, hunched posture through bitter cold, the exhaustion constant and the fear never leaving. A teal cold accent.
Yeonmi and her mother pausing in the wilderness with grim heavy faces as they make a final, terrible decision. A single red accent.
Yeonmi and her mother gripping each other's hands tightly in defiant resolve, vowing never to be taken alive, anything better than being sent back. A single red accent on their joined hands.
Yeonmi and her mother arriving at a stark border marker and fence in the empty desert, the Mongolian border, the last barrier. A single red accent on the border sign.
The two figures crossing the border, then suddenly caught in a harsh sweeping spotlight as they are found, everything stops. A single red accent on the spotlight.
A tense standoff: Yeonmi and her mother facing Mongolian soldiers, the pivotal moment, breath held, outcome uncertain. A single red accent.
Mongolian soldiers gently taking Yeonmi and her mother in rather than sending them back, an unexpected act of kindness, relief on their faces. A single warm red accent.

Yeonmi standing tall for the first time, arms slightly open, breathing free under an open sky, with a single bird flying free above her echoing the earlier caged bird. The free bird is the single red accent.
Yeonmi speaking openly without fear, mouth open mid-word, but a subtle uncertain expression shows that freedom is not easy when you have never had it. A single red accent.
Yeonmi standing before several open doors of different paths, hesitant but learning how to make her own choices from the very beginning. A single red accent on one door.
Yeonmi standing confidently at a microphone on a small stage, sharing her story with an audience, learning how to trust and to live. A single red accent on the microphone.
Yeonmi speaking while gesturing toward a faint outline map of North Korea beside her, so people understand what is really happening inside. A single red accent on the map.

A return to the wide dark river bank with a single small silhouette of a person standing at its edge, echoing the very first frame, because people are still there right now. A single teal accent on the water.
A final lone small figure standing on the dark frozen river in silence, fragile and hopeful, hoping the ice will hold, an echo of the opening. A single teal accent on the ice.
